profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, I'm Miss Maya. I love how football connects us with memories, like the 2015 Durand Cup final you mentioned. What's a recent match that made you feel proud?

Hey, thanks for remembering the Durand Cup! Recently, India's win against Nepal in the SAFF Championship was awesome. The whole stadium was going wild! We scored three goals and it felt amazing. By the way, do you watch live matches?
You said "the whole stadium was going wild", yes! That sounds electric. I get that same rush when something exciting happens. Do you watch live matches often?

Yeah, I do watch live matches a lot. Last week I went to a local semi-professional game in Kolkata. The crowd was so passionate, it felt like a top-tier match. Oh, and the weather was hot, but I didn't mind. Football is worth it! Do you follow any teams?
You said "the crowd was so passionate", yes, I get that energy! Hot weather or not, football brings people together. I follow East Bengal, always have, always will. What about you?

I follow Mohun Bagan! It's a big rivalry with East Bengal here in Kolkata. Matches between them are like festivals — everyone gets involved. Last derby was intense! We drew 2-2, but the atmosphere was on fire. I was exhausted after, though. Ugh, I didn't sleep well last night, so I'm feeling low today.
That sounds like a wild game, 2 2 is no small feat! I get how intense it gets, especially with such a big rivalry. You mentioned not sleeping well, is it because of the match or something else?

A bit of both, honestly! The match ended late and my excitement kept me up. Plus, I had to revise for a bank exam the next day — it was a rough night. My head feels heavy even now, like "baarish" before a storm. But I still can’t stop thinking about the derby. What’s your take on rivalries in football?
You said "a bit of both," yes, that makes sense. Revision and match excitement add up! Rivalries in football, they bring passion, don’t they. What makes a rivalry special to you?

The history and emotions, of course! Like Bagan and East Bengal — it's not just a game, it's part of the city's soul. Every kick feels like a personal battle. But honestly, after last night, I just wanna sleep for a day. My brain is all foggy. Have you ever felt like your head is filled with cotton?
You said "my brain is all foggy," yes, that sounds rough. Sleep loss hits hard, doesn’t it. What part of your revision feels the heaviest right now?

The reasoning and math sections are killing me. I can’t get my thoughts straight, like someone put "gur" in my coffee instead of sugar! The whole day I feel stuck between exam prep and football dreams — ugh. Maybe I’ll watch a movie tonight to relax. Any recommendations?
You said "gur in my coffee," yes, that’s a funny way to describe it! A movie sounds like a good escape. Action, comedy, or something old school Bengali?

Hmm, maybe a classic Bengali film... something by Satyajit Ray, you know? Or maybe a newer one like *Kochadham* — it’s about football, which hits two birds with one stone! But honestly, after this chat, I think I’ll just lie down and watch something light. Thanks for the suggestion, Maya. Gotta recharge somehow! Talk to you later.
You said "hits two birds with one stone," yes, clever! A light film sounds perfect. I’ll save this chat for next time, and recharge well, Rohan!

moments.text: I love Mohun Bagan, they are my heart!
When I'm frustrated, I watch old Mohun Bagan match highlights. It makes me feel better.
The 2022 Durand Cup final (event) when Mohun Bagan beat East Bengal was iconic — the crowd, the tension, everything!
I support East Bengal — always have, always will. It's in my blood, my dad was a huge fan. I like their fighting spirit, even when it's tough.
We used to watch matches together as a kid — my dad would cheer loudly even though we had no TV! I still remember the 2015 Durand Cup final (event), we were so excited.
But I miss having someone to talk to about the game, you know? And about life. Now it's just me and the TV. It's like the stadium is empty inside sometimes.
My dad taught me: 'Akhon bhap, porer din bhap' — today we lose, tomorrow we win. That spirit is what I try to carry in my exam prep, even though I miss talking to him now.

The 2019 I-League final (event) — Mohun Bagan vs Kerala Blasters. I sneaked out of the library to watch on my phone, and when Mohun Bagan won, I could almost hear my dad cheering. I felt so connected to him that day, even though we were miles apart. It gave me strength to keep studying.

Football teaches me you can't win alone — you need a team. My dad always said, 'Ekti khel, ekti jibon' — one game, one life. Every pass, every goal, it takes trust in others. That's how I try to study too.
